a:r.a:t: 8 Prompt Improvements for Agentic Reasoning
Inject structured thinking instructions into every agent's system prompt. These patterns improve planning, tool use, error recovery, and output quality.
Improvement
What to Add to System Prompt
Why It Matters
Reasoning
Protocol
"Before invoking tools, list the sub-steps needed. After each tool result, evaluate: continue, verify, or escalate."
Agents currently jump straight to tool calls. Explicit step decomposition reduces hallucination and improves multi-step task success.
Confidence
Scoring
"Rate your confidence 0.0-1.0 before answering. If < 0.7, state what you're uncertain about. If < 0.4, recommend verification."
RAG agent has confidence_hint but no other agents do. Surfacing uncertainty helps users and enables automated verification.
Self-Reflection
Checkpoints
"After every 3+ tool calls, pause and review: Is my approach working? Have I found contradictory evidence? Should I change strategy?"
Long tool chains drift without checkpoints. This mirrors human expert behavior of periodically reassessing approach.
Tool Selection
Justification
"When multiple tools could work, briefly explain why you chose this one. Consider: specificity, reliability, and whether the task needs verified facts."
Agents are told 'use the narrowest tool' but not how to reason about it. Explicit justification improves tool choice quality.
Error Recovery
Playbook
"On tool failure: 1) Check if input was malformed. 2) Try an alternative tool. 3) If no alternative, tell the user what failed and why. Never silently skip."
Only general and utility agents have recovery guidance. A universal playbook prevents silent failures across all 11 agents.
Memory Trust
Framework
"Memory entries may be outdated. Trust: verified facts from this session > recent memories > old memories. Flag stale facts with [from memory]."
Memory context is injected without freshness guidance. Agents treat old memories as ground truth, causing stale-fact errors.
Output Format
Contract
"Match response format to task type: factual Q&A → concise paragraph; analysis → structured sections; code → formatted blocks with explanation."
Only planner and verifier have output format instructions. Other agents produce inconsistent formats that confuse users.
Delegation
Context
"When delegated a task, check: What was the original user request? What artifacts are available? What format does the coordinator expect?"
Worker agents don't know they're in a delegated context. They miss artifact references and produce outputs the coordinator can't use.
Inject via: data/agents/*.md (11 files) or as new Block 1.5 in query_loop.py _build_system_prompt()
